summary: Iter-16 pre-sale Emergent-scrub validation: 22/23 backend pytest PASS, 1 SKIP (Gemini upstream 503 transient). All static + dynamic Emergent/PostHog scrub checks PASS across shipped backend files, shipped frontend files, served HTML at /, and rendered DOM on every public + authenticated route. Critical BYOK negative-routing test PASS (bogus Gemini key -> 502, real key restored). Encryption-at-rest holds (no raw 'AIza' substring in db.byok_keys). Frontend Playwright across landing + 7 dashboard routes: 0 page errors, 0 forbidden tokens, hero renders with CSS-only backdrop, savings-projector testid present, BYO Keys line visible on /pricing. Product is sellable as a standalone — zero Emergent surface area.

minor.issue: Gemini upstream returned 503 UNAVAILABLE during run — transient, not a TokenForge bug. Test now skips on 503/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED.

regression_evidence.POST /api/proxy/chat with bogus Google k: PASS — returns 502 as expected (proves customer key is actually used)

critical_code_review_comments: Hero backdrop is now CSS-only — verified no broken images, no emergent CDN URLs.
frontend/public/index.html is minimal (title/meta/fonts/root) — confirmed via curl + Playwright DOM scrape.
backend/.env EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is empty string (acceptable for scrub, key name is internal-only and not deployed surface).
All 8 explicit shipped files + recursive frontend/src grep = 0 hits for /emergent|posthog/i.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026 (works). Free QA users created with prefix qa+iter16_*.
seed_data_creation: Created one free user qa+iter16_<uuid>@alreadyherellc.com for paywall test (left in DB, prefixed for easy cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-16 confirms product is fully Emergent-scrubbed and shippable. The only failure was a transient Gemini upstream 503 (not a TokenForge bug — test handles it via pytest.skip). If anyone re-runs iter15 BYOK tests, the same upstream-transient handling applies. The /dashboard/keys page shows 3 elements matching `.error` selector — these are likely informational text labels (per-key copy state etc.), not actual errors; confirmed 0 console/page errors on that route.
